title: Feedback-backed evaluations can complete without usable RCA payload
description:
## Summary
Feedback-backed evaluations can currently be marked complete even when a usable RCA payload is absent or never persisted.

## Symptom
A feedback-backed evaluation may complete without usable `root_cause` output even when incorrect items exist.

## Impact
This is a process-critical reliability defect because RCA is required to make evaluation results actionable in the optimization loop.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Starting implementation: enforce feedback-backed completion contract requiring persisted non-empty root_cause when incorrect items exist; RCA default-on in feedback paths; add regression tests.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Implemented: feedback-backed runs now fail when incorrect items exist but no usable root_cause payload is persisted. Added regression tests in Evaluation_feedback_test for fail-on-missing-RCA and pass-when-no-incorrect-items.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Validation note: shell environment lacks pytest ( reports module not found). Performed syntax validation via  on all changed Python files.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Validation note correction: pytest is not installed in this shell environment, so unit tests could not be executed here. Syntax validation completed with python3 -m py_compile for all changed Python files.
